learning_objectives.text: State the purpose of the Proton H2 Safety Orientation and identify the knowledge areas it covers.
List the five key outcomes a worker should be able to demonstrate upon completing this orientation.

blocks.alt: Occupational Health and Safety Orientation title slide

blocks.text: Hazards exist in every workplace. Everyone needs to be aware of these hazards, and the potential risks they may pose. Safety training helps ensure that you have the knowledge to work safely every day, because you have the right to a safe and healthy workplace.

The Purpose of this Safety Orientation is to provide you, as an employee or contractor of Proton H2, with the knowledge of basic Health and Safety Regulations, Responsibilities and Safe Work Practices that apply to all locations operated by Proton H2.
By the end of this Orientation, you will be able to:

items: Locate Proton H2's Health and Safety Policy, and be able to reference its content
Locate and be able to reference your safety management systems
Understand the basic requirements for health and safety on Proton H2 work sites

learning_objectives.text: Describe the core expectations and accountabilities for all Proton H2 employees regarding health and safety performance.
Identify the HSE commitments outlined in the Proton H2 Health and Safety Policy and explain where the policy can be found.
Locate the Proton H2 Health and Safety Program Manual and understand each employee's responsibility to be familiar with its contents.

blocks.alt: The Health and Safety Program section title slide

blocks.text: All employees of Proton H2 are expected to follow the health and safety guidelines as set out in the safety manual and safety program.
Everyone is accountable for their health and safety performance.

blocks.title: Incident Reporting
blocks.text: All accidents/incidents/near misses must be reported immediately and will be investigated.
In support of this Policy, Proton H2 has identified the following HSE commitments:

items: Maintain and continuously improve our comprehensive HSE Management System (HSE Management System)
Provide resources, training and technology to meet our HSE goals, objectives, targets and commitments.
Strive to eliminate any foreseeable hazards
Safeguard the environment, including prevention and the mitigation of environmental impacts
Ensure all levels of management and supervisors comply with HSE responsibilities and requirements.
Ensure all workers perform their jobs properly in accordance with established responsibilities, policies, standards, codes of practice, safe work practices, and safe operating procedures
Comply with all applicable HSE legislation
Establish maximum cooperation based on a strong sense of personal responsibility.

blocks.title: HSE Policy Location
blocks.text: Proton H2 Health, Safety and Environmental (HSE) Policy can be found at the beginning of the Health and Safety Program Manual.

items: The Proton H2 Health and Safety Program Manual can be found online or as a binder in the office/onsite
Ensure you know where to find your safety manual
Read through all relevant sections and information
Ask questions if you are not sure about content or other information in the safety program
Every employee has the responsibility to be familiar with this program and know where to access it.

blocks.alt: Health and Safety Program Manual reference illustration
Health and Safety Program Manual reference illustration 2

learning_objectives.text: Identify and describe the hierarchy of hazard controls used in risk assessments, from elimination through to PPE.

rows: Substituting something to lessen or eliminate the identified hazard(s)
Engineering the hazard (enclosures, guards, barriers, different tools/equipment)
Changes in procedures, policies or processes
Personal protective equipment — providing protective equipment to create a barrier between the worker and the hazards (gloves, safety glasses, hard hats, steel toed boots etc.)
blocks.caption: Hierarchy of hazard controls applied in risk assessments

blocks.description: Hazards exist in every workplace and pose potential risks to workers if not identified and controlled through risk assessment.

controls.text: Eliminate the hazard completely or engineer controls such as enclosures, guards, and barriers
Substitute hazardous materials or processes and implement changes in procedures, policies, or processes
Provide personal protective equipment such as gloves, safety glasses, hard hats, and steel toed boots to create a barrier between the worker and the hazard

blocks.alt: Risk assessments and hazard controls illustration

learning_objectives.text: Explain the responsibilities of owners/employers and workers under provincial OH&S legislation as it applies to Proton H2 worksites.

blocks.text: Every Provincial legislation states, in same or similar verbiage, the responsibility of the owner/employer to do everything practicable to ensure the health, safety and welfare of all workers working on applicable worksites. They must also ensure workers are aware of their rights and duties.

Workers must ensure they understand the relevant OH&S legislation, and take reasonable care and attention to protect the health and safety of all workers and others in or in the vicinity of work being done by Proton H2 and its contractors.

learning_objectives.text: Locate and record Proton H2 head office contact details, including the 24-hour emergency number.

blocks.caption: Proton H2 head office and emergency contact details

blocks.title: 24-Hour Emergency Line
blocks.text: In the event of an emergency, contact the 24 Hour Emergency Number: 1 (877) 000 0000.
